rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: The test spins a REAL reposix-sim axum process in-process (reposix_sim::run_with_listener) backed by a persistent SQLite file, pre-seeds it via the sim's own production reposix_sim::seed::apply_seed, drives a real push through git-remote-reposix, then opens that SAME sqlite file with rusqlite::Connection::open and queries audit_events directly (COUNT(*) WHERE method=... AND path=...) -- not a wiremock request-log proxy. If the backend-mutation audit row were silently dropped, patch_count would read 0 and the assert_eq!(patch_count, 1, ...) would fail concretely, naming the missing count. Run + confirmed PASS on this host (git 2.25.1) 2026-07-05 -- this scenario never touches git>=2.34 codepaths (plain git init/commit/push against a file:// mirror + the export capability), so it is NOT gated by the git-version floor.
